title: Confusion Matrices & Error Analysis
concept: A confusion matrix displays correct and incorrect predictions across all classes, exposing specific pattern errors.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What type of error does a model make when it fails to detect a target that is actually present?
exercises.explain: Correct! Missing a positive target is a False Negative, which hurts Recall.

cards.title: Analyzing the mistakes
cards.body: If your car navigation system occasionally gets lost, you want to know: is it getting lost everywhere, or does it specifically get confused at a certain highway intersection? A confusion matrix maps out exactly where a model gets lost.

cards.title: The Confusion Matrix
cards.body: A confusion matrix is a table layout that visualizes the performance of a classifier. Each row of the matrix represents instances in an actual class, while each column represents instances in a predicted class (or vice versa), showing exactly which classes are confused.

cards.title: A grid of intersections
cards.body: Think of a city map grid. The rows are where drivers wanted to go, and the columns are where they actually ended up. If everyone trying to go to 'North Station' ends up at 'West Station', the map grid highlights that specific wrong turn instantly.

cards.title: Wolf vs. Husky confusion
cards.body: A 3x3 confusion matrix for Cat, Dog, Wolf shows that Cat is rarely misclassified. However, out of 100 actual Wolves, 30 are predicted as Dogs. This tells the engineer they need more distinctive wolf-ear features.

cards.title: Diagonal is not the only focus
cards.body: You might think you should only look at the diagonal of the matrix, which shows correct predictions. In reality, the off-diagonal cells are the most valuable for error analysis, exposing systemic biases in the model's feature extraction.

cards.title: Confusion Matrix Layout
cards.body: How actual vs. predicted values intersect:

left.text:
Actual: Dog | Predicted: Dog
Correct classifications
We want high numbers here

right.text:
Actual: Wolf | Predicted: Dog
Systematic mistakes
Shows model confusion

exercises.prompt: What do the off-diagonal elements in a confusion matrix represent?
exercises.explain: Off-diagonal cells represent errors — instances where actual class A was predicted as class B.

options.text: Misclassifications where the predicted label did not match the actual label
Perfect predictions where actual and predicted match
The total number of parameters in the model

exercises.prompt: True or false: A confusion matrix is only useful for binary (2-class) classification problems.
exercises.explain: False. Confusion matrices can be scaled to any number of classes (N x N), displaying complex relationships between multiple categories.
The diagonal cells represent intersections where actual and predicted classes are identical.
exercises.sentence: In a confusion matrix, correct predictions lie along the ___ of the grid.

exercises.prompt: Which of these findings is best identified using a confusion matrix?
exercises.explain: Exposing specific, class-to-class confusions is the primary utility of a confusion matrix.

options.text: The model frequently confuses roses with tulips, but rarely with trees
The model's average training loss is 0.45
The validation images are too low in resolution

exercises.prompt: You review a 3x3 confusion matrix for [Car, Truck, Motorcycle]. The model has 90% overall accuracy, but the row for 'Truck' shows 40% of actual trucks are predicted as 'Car'. How do you resolve this?

options.text: Gather more diverse training images of trucks, especially from angles that highlight truck beds and height, to help the model tell them apart from cars.
Delete the car class from the training set.
Change the activation function of the final layer to ReLU.
Run the model on higher resolution validation files.

exercises.explain: Exactly! The confusion matrix isolated the problem: the model is blind to the difference between trucks and cars (likely due to shared features like wheels and windshields). Focused data collection targeting truck-specific features is the correct solution. Next, let's look at ROC and PR curves!
